title: India Signs $629 Million BrahMos Missile Deal with Vietnam at Shangri-La Dialogue
summary: India finalised a $629 million deal to supply Vietnam with BrahMos Block 3 supersonic cruise missiles at the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore. A similar deal with Indonesia is in its final stages. The deals represent a landmark in India's defence export ambitions.

coverages.headline: India Finalises $629 Million BrahMos Block 3 Missile Deal With Vietnam at Shangri-La Dialogue
coverages.excerpt: India signed a landmark defence export deal with Vietnam at the Shangri-La Dialogue, with a similar Indonesia deal in final stages — a major boost to India's defence export ambitions.
coverages.bias_notes: Defence export deals are typically covered as wins for the exporting country. What this framing doesn't address: Vietnam's motivation (deterrence against China in the South China Sea), the geopolitical signal it sends to Beijing, and India's own diplomatic calculation in arming a country with an active territorial dispute with China. The deal is strategic, not just commercial.

coverages.headline: BHEL and SAIL risk losing Maharatna status over three consecutive years of losses
coverages.excerpt: Two of India's premier PSUs face downgrade to Navratna after failing to meet the ₹5,000 crore PAT criterion over three consecutive years, potentially limiting their financial autonomy.
coverages.bias_notes: The BHEL and SAIL Maharatna story sits in sharp contrast to the BrahMos export success: India's defence export ambitions are growing while two of its largest public sector enterprises are financially struggling. The PSU downgrade story gets almost no coverage relative to the defence deal, even though it has more direct implications for thousands of workers and the government's balance sheet.
